channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19278] Null pointer deref exception with osd_attr_get
item.description:
CX recently encountered this issue where they run many of their OSTs to full capacity and 2 OSS out of ~300 OSSs saw this crash.

[442601.088522] LustreError: 1038502:0:(tgt_grant.c:472:tgt_grant_space_left()) lustrefs-OST00a4: cli 55d74450-4b3f-415c-a830-9d3f4126f1d5/000000002ee65e21 left=529690624 < tot_grant=6756016175 unstable=0 pending=0 dirty=8417280
[442601.088528] LustreError: 1038502:0:(tgt_grant.c:472:tgt_grant_space_left()) Skipped 20975 previous similar messages
[442710.230093] LustreError: 287529:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) lustrefs-OST00a4: unable to precreate: rc = -28
[442710.230098] LustreError: 287529:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) Skipped 3 previous similar messages
[442973.953697] LustreError: 367776:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) lustrefs-OST00a4: unable to precreate: rc = -28
[442973.953702] LustreError: 367776:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) Skipped 5 previous similar messages
[442990.802602] Lustre: 308494:0:(osd_handler.c:586:osd_ldiskfs_add_entry()) lustrefs-OST00a4: directory (inode: 157548816, FID: [0x9640110:0xabb62bd4:0x0]) has reached max size limit
[442991.305415] BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000058
[442991.306436] PGD 0 
[442991.306687] Oops: 0000 [#1] SMP NOPTI
[442991.307118] CPU: 161 PID: 2199701 Comm: ll_ost09_049 Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE  X  -------- -  - 4.18.0-553.5.1.el8_10_lustre_oci_250709.x86_64 #1
[442991.308869] Hardware name: QEMU Standard PC (Q35 + ICH9, 2009), BIOS 1.6.4 02/27/2023
[442991.309812] RIP: 0010:osd_attr_get+0xa1/0x700 [osd_ldiskfs]
[442991.310495] Code: 00 01 0f 85 42 03 00 00 a8 02 0f 85 50 03 00 00 4d 8d b5 a8 00 00 00 4c 89 f7 e8 2a 6d b8 d1 49 8b 6d 40 48 81 0b ff 9f 08 00 <48> 8b 45 58 48 89 43 18 48 8b 45 68 48 89 43 10 48 8b 45 78 48 89
[442991.312658] RSP: 0018:ff78785742fffbf0 EFLAGS: 00010206
[442991.313284] RAX: 0000000000000000 RBX: ff4eb835f2f86c30 RCX: ff4eb831602eb530
[442991.314121] RDX: 0000000000000001 RSI: ff4eb8320c953c00 RDI: ff4eb8320c953ca8
[442991.314956] RBP: 0000000000000000 R08: ff7878574f615ab9 R09: 0000000000000000
[442991.315796] R10: ff4eb837ed2ac600 R11: 0000000000000100 R12: ff4eb8382172bc80
[442991.316646] R13: ff4eb8320c953c00 R14: ff4eb8320c953ca8 R15: ff4eb835f2f86c00
[442991.317479] FS:  0000000000000000(0000) GS:ff4eb932a6440000(0000) knlGS:0000000000000000
[442991.318433] CS:  0010 DS: 0000 ES: 0000 CR0: 0000000080050033
[442991.319095] CR2: 0000000000000058 CR3: 00000148de810003 CR4: 0000000000771ee0
[442991.319946] PKRU: 55555554
[442991.320275] Call Trace:
[442991.320570]  ? __die_body+0x1a/0x60
[442991.320987]  ? no_context+0x1ba/0x3f0
[442991.321421]  ? __bad_area_nosemaphore+0x16c/0x1c0
[442991.321982]  ? do_page_fault+0x37/0x12d
[442991.322442]  ? page_fault+0x1e/0x30
[442991.322863]  ? osd_attr_get+0xa1/0x700 [osd_ldiskfs]
[442991.323481]  ofd_attr_get+0x93/0x2a0 [ofd]
[442991.323965]  ofd_lvbo_init+0x595/0x9e0 [ofd]
[442991.324484]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.325060]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.325629]  ? ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0xe27/0x1500 [ptlrpc]
[442991.326336]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.326904]  ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0xe27/0x1500 [ptlrpc]
[442991.327562]  tgt_enqueue+0xa4/0x220 [ptlrpc]
[442991.328122]  tgt_request_handle+0xccd/0x1a20 [ptlrpc]
[442991.328763]  ? ptlrpc_nrs_req_get_nolock0+0xff/0x1f0 [ptlrpc]
[442991.329510]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.330099]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbe0 [ptlrpc]
[442991.330847]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.331430]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.331983]  ptlrpc_main+0xbec/0x1530 [ptlrpc]
[442991.332566]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442991.333160]  ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0x590/0x590 [ptlrpc]
[442991.333799]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[442991.334214]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[442991.334716]  ret_from_fork+0x1f/0x40
item.summary: Null pointer deref exception with osd_attr_get

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Alex Zhuravlev

I still think it would be just a good check for NULL inode before get_attr call, in case we find there could be other situation where osd_object is not dropped when it should have?

Looks like it
Thanks Alex
I think you need https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55571

Hmm make sense.
The thing is - not one but 2 OSTs in this filesystem met with same fate. Another one OST000c had exact same stack trace after the osd_ldiskfs_add_entry() error

[442576.303721] LustreError: 514145:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) lustrefs-OST000c: unable to precreate: rc = -28
[442576.303726] LustreError: 514145:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) Skipped 3 previous similar messages
[442839.822368] LustreError: 285583:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) lustrefs-OST000c: unable to precreate: rc = -28
[442839.822373] LustreError: 285583:0:(ofd_dev.c:1740:ofd_create_hdl()) Skipped 6 previous similar messages
[442949.938330] Lustre: 36285:0:(osd_handler.c:586:osd_ldiskfs_add_entry()) lustrefs-OST000c: directory (inode: 157548681, FID: [0x9640089:0x74b3e048:0x0]) has reached max size limit
[442950.192938] BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000058
[442950.194013] PGD 0 
[442950.194273] Oops: 0000 [#1] SMP NOPTI
[442950.194713] CPU: 174 PID: 2201310 Comm: ll_ost10_015 Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE  X  -------- -  - 4.18.0-553.5.1.el8_10_lustre_oci_250709.x86_64 #1
[442950.196370] Hardware name: QEMU Standard PC (Q35 + ICH9, 2009), BIOS 1.6.4 02/27/2023
[442950.197290] RIP: 0010:osd_attr_get+0xa1/0x700 [osd_ldiskfs]
[442950.197957] Code: 00 01 0f 85 42 03 00 00 a8 02 0f 85 50 03 00 00 4d 8d b5 a8 00 00 00 4c 89 f7 e8 2a 3d 53 ea 49 8b 6d 40 48 81 0b ff 9f 08 00 <48> 8b 45 58 48 89 43 18 48 8b 45 68 48 89 43 10 48 8b 45 78 48 89
[442950.200135] RSP: 0018:ff623fcd40007bf0 EFLAGS: 00010206
[442950.200761] RAX: 0000000000000000 RBX: ff1e7ddf19af4c30 RCX: ff1e7dddc46fb630
[442950.201603] RDX: 0000000000000001 RSI: ff1e7de0ee2a1a00 RDI: ff1e7de0ee2a1aa8
[442950.202430] RBP: 0000000000000000 R08: ff623fcd4f9f3e41 R09: 0000000000000000
[442950.203260] R10: ff1e7de02f39f600 R11: 000192df5cbd9000 R12: ff1e7de03b8bfc40
[442950.204117] R13: ff1e7de0ee2a1a00 R14: ff1e7de0ee2a1aa8 R15: ff1e7ddf19af4c00
[442950.204949] FS:  0000000000000000(0000) GS:ff1e7edee6780000(0000) knlGS:0000000000000000
[442950.205889] CS:  0010 DS: 0000 ES: 0000 CR0: 0000000080050033
[442950.206573] CR2: 0000000000000058 CR3: 00000181ce810002 CR4: 0000000000771ee0
[442950.207416] PKRU: 55555554
[442950.207748] Call Trace:
[442950.208053]  ? __die_body+0x1a/0x60
[442950.208489]  ? no_context+0x1ba/0x3f0
[442950.208930]  ? __bad_area_nosemaphore+0x16c/0x1c0
[442950.209489]  ? do_page_fault+0x37/0x12d
[442950.209958]  ? page_fault+0x1e/0x30
[442950.210401]  ? osd_attr_get+0xa1/0x700 [osd_ldiskfs]
[442950.210992]  ofd_attr_get+0x93/0x2a0 [ofd]
[442950.211486]  ofd_lvbo_init+0x595/0x9e0 [ofd]
[442950.212002]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.212582]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.213171]  ? ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0xe27/0x1500 [ptlrpc]
[442950.213870]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.214441]  ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0xe27/0x1500 [ptlrpc]
[442950.215102]  tgt_enqueue+0xa4/0x220 [ptlrpc]
[442950.215676]  tgt_request_handle+0xccd/0x1a20 [ptlrpc]
[442950.216340]  ? ptlrpc_nrs_req_get_nolock0+0xff/0x1f0 [ptlrpc]
[442950.217066]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.217641]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbe0 [ptlrpc]
[442950.218378]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.218933]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.219499]  ptlrpc_main+0xbec/0x1530 [ptlrpc]
[442950.220073]  ? srso_alias_return_thunk+0x5/0xfcdfd
[442950.220658]  ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0x590/0x590 [ptlrpc]
[442950.221296]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[442950.221708]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[442950.222213]  ret_from_fork+0x1f/0x40

and then, if LOHA_EXIST is not set, then osd_attr_get() exits right away:

	if (unlikely(!dt_object_exists(dt)))
		return -ENOENT;

Thanks @Alex Zhuravlev for reviewing, appreciate your prompt response on the patch.

I was wondering, in osd_object_init(), if this condition is false then it will skip setting LOHA_EXISTS

if (result == 0 && obj->oo_inode != NULL)
    if (OBD_FAIL_PRECHECK(OBD_FAIL_MDS_LLOG_UMOUNT_RACE) &&
        cfs_fail_val == 2) {
        struct osd_thread_info *info = osd_oti_get(env);
        struct osd_idmap_cache *oic = &info->oti_cache;
        /* invalidate thread cache */
        memset(&oic->oic_fid, 0, sizeof(oic->oic_fid));
    }
    if (fid_is_otable_it(&l->lo_header->loh_fid)) {
        obj->oo_dt.do_ops = &osd_obj_otable_it_ops;
        l->lo_header->loh_attr |= LOHA_EXISTS;
        return 0;
    }    result = osd_fid_lookup(env, obj, lu_object_fid(l), conf);

    obj->oo_dt.do_body_ops = &osd_body_ops_new;

    if (result == 0 && obj->oo_inode != NULL) { <-----------------------------
        struct osd_thread_info *oti = osd_oti_get(env);
        struct lustre_ost_attrs *loa = &oti->oti_ost_attrs;
        osd_object_init0(obj);
        if (unlikely(obj->oo_header))
            return 0;

   }

I believe at the time of creation, the osd objects always have inode set to NULL which then later gets updated once the ldiskfs call for the object completes. In this case, looks like ldiskfs call didn't succeed and still osd object wasn't cleared

if an object creation fails, then no LOHA_EXIST is set

"Sonia Sharma <sonia.sh.sharma@oracle.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62391
Subject: LU-19278 osd: check null inode in osd_attr_get
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 76618afa2a35a28c5377c63f646cef6c1f145041
@Alex Zhuravlev I would really appreciate if you can share any thoughts you might have here. Thank you

The interesting thing in the logs I found was that though we see the "reached max size limit" message but we never see the "is approaching max size limit" from osd_ldisks_add_entry()

[442990.802602] Lustre: 308494:0:(osd_handler.c:586:osd_ldiskfs_add_entry()) lustrefs-OST00a4: directory (inode: 157548816, FID: [0x9640110:0xabb62bd4:0x0]) has reached max size limit

Had verified that large_dir is enabled

dumpe2fs 1.47.1-wc1 (28-May-2024)
Filesystem volume name:   lustrefs-MDT0000
Last mounted on:          /
Filesystem UUID:          10aaa27d-efe3-4672-b8be-0be2711bcec6
Filesystem magic number:  0xEF53
Filesystem revision #:    1 (dynamic)
Filesystem features:      has_journal ext_attr resize_inode dir_index filetype needs_recovery mmp flex_bg ea_inode dirdata large_dir sparse_super large_file huge_file uninit_bg dir_nlink quota project

I see this issue created but they had not enabled large_dir enabled. Also looks like they were seeing both the messages from -ENOBUFS and -ENOSPC errors.

So I am thinking, this issue is different. Are we seeing hash collision and that is why ext4 returning just ENOSPC without ever reporting ENOBUFS?
Has such an issue seen before?

Debugging the coredump and see that the inode is NULL.
So it kind of understood now that the crash happened because of osd_get_attr()->osd_inode_getattr() with inode value being NULL.

One quick fix I can do is to have a NULL check of inode before accessing it. 
At the same time, trying to root cause why osd_attr_get() was called for the object with NULL inode.

I believe at the time of creation, the osd objects always have inode set to NULL which then later gets updated once the ldiskfs call for the object completes. In this case, looks like ldiskfs call didn't succeed and still osd object wasn't cleared

crash> struct osd_object ff4eb8320c953c00
struct osd_object {
  oo_dt = {
    do_lu = {
      lo_header = 0xff4eb837ed2ac600,
      lo_dev = 0xff4eb834a5160000,
      lo_ops = 0xffffffffc1add440 <osd_lu_obj_ops>,
      lo_linkage = {
        next = 0xff4eb837ed2ac638,
        prev = 0xff4eb837ed2ac670
      },
      lo_dev_ref = {<No data fields>}
    },
    do_ops = 0xffffffffc1add320 <osd_obj_ops>,
    do_body_ops = 0xffffffffc1ade780 <osd_body_ops>,
    do_index_ops = 0x0
  },
  oo_inode = 0x0,    <--------------------
  oo_hl_head = 0x0,
  oo_ext_idx_sem = {
    count = {
      counter = 0
    },
    wait_list = {
      next = 0xff4eb8320c953c58,
      prev = 0xff4eb8320c953c58
    },
